text: Moving from the dim hallway into this display, I feel a sense of anticipation and curiosity. The low lighting, glass doors, and the almost clinical silence of the first scene gave me a vague unease—an awareness of boundaries and the unknown. Entering the second space, I am greeted by ideas of experimentation, research, and poetic confusion. The wall embraces not knowing, and I sense a comforting invitation to explore and accept ambiguity. Here, confusion is not failure—it’s possibility. I feel both disoriented and inspired.
